title: Opening Keynote, Michael Truell | Compile 26
summary: At Cursor's inaugural Compile conference, CEO Michael Truell announced Cursor Mobile, Origin Git platform, cloud agent updates, and a new model trained from scratch. The platform reports 95% usage is now agents, and Cursor is positioning itself as an agent-native development environment by giving agents their own compute resources.
writeup: The opening keynote of Cursor's first Compile conference, delivered by CEO Michael Truell, covers a set of product announcements: Cursor Mobile, an Origin Git platform, cloud agent updates, and a model trained from scratch. Truell frames Cursor as an agent-native development environment, citing that 95% of platform usage now comes from agents and that agents are being given their own compute resources. The announcements matter because they signal a shift from a code editor toward infrastructure built around autonomous agents, including a Git platform and dedicated agent compute rather than only assisted editing.
